misconceptionSummary: Students continue a geometric sequence by adding the last visible difference instead of multiplying.

steps.narration: The pattern is multiplication by three.
So the next term should be fifty-four, not thirty.

lines.text: Adding 12 copies the last difference, not the rule

steps.narration: The student copied a difference instead of the multiplier.

visuals.expression: Use the constant multiplier, not the changing differences
visuals.label: Geometric continuation needs multiplication

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, check the ratio between terms.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: using an additive rule on a multiplicative sequence. Check how one term is multiplied to get the next.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If the sequence is geometric, addition will usually give the wrong continuation.
